addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  Largest implementation pace — fenestrate transition plus new phase 3. Handbook recast moved to ₢A-ABF (parallelizable).
  
  ## Goal
  Build caparison-windows verb per BUSJCW: phases 1+2 (SSH-trust hardening, currently inhabited by fenestrate) and phase 3 (WSL stage, powercfg sleep disable, Tailscale auto-start). Delete fenestrate bash implementation.
  
  ## Boundary
  - caparison-windows is the new entry; fenestrate disappears from bash: function family, dispatcher, `buw-jpF` tabtarget, zipper enrollment.
  - Phase 3 ops are new bash; phases 1+2 carry forward existing logic re-homed under the new verb.
  - Closes with invigilate-windows post-completion check (depends on ₢A-ABD, already landed).
  - Handbook rewrite is out of scope — owned by ₢A-ABF.
  
  ## Done when
  - `buw-jpCW` exists; runs phases 1-3 idempotently end-to-end.
  - `grep -ri fenestrate` over `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `bujb_cli.sh`, `buwz_zipper.sh`, `bujp_preflight.sh`, and `tt/` returns zero.
  - bash -n; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh.

40effad0  ₢A-ABE    Close the operator-precondition spec gap surfaced during ₢A-ABD by extending BUSJI{L,M} with handbook-scope precondition facts and migrating the corresponding probes from bujp_preflight into the invigilate verbs. BUSJIL gains two steps after tailscaled-active: sudo NOPASSWD available (`sudo -n true` exit 0) and sudo scope covers garrison commands (`sudo -ln userdel` exit 0), both fatal pointing to BUSJHL scope. BUSJIM gains three steps after tailscaled PID live: admin-group membership (`dseditgroup -o checkmember -m <user> admin`), sudo NOPASSWD available, and sudo scope (`sudo -ln sysadminctl`), all fatal pointing to BUSJHM scope. All new steps follow the existing busc_call/busc_store/busc_require/busc_fatal shape — no new operation kinds introduced. Scope-probe exemplars match garrison-bash's actual sudo callsites in bujb_jurisdiction.sh (userdel for linux, sysadminctl for mac), so the scope assertion is a real future-failure prediction rather than a synthetic check. bujb_invigilate_macos and bujb_invigilate_linux extended with matching zbujb_invigilate_fact_die-shaped fact blocks reusing the function-local z_exit and the ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_{STDOUT,STDERR} kindle constants; pointer strings include BUSJHL/BUSJHM callouts so the diagnostic lands the operator at the recovery copy. bujp_preflight.sh dropped from 309 to ~125 lines: deleted zbujp_probe_sudo, zbujp_probe_admin_group_mac, zbujp_diag_sudo_missing (the elaborate scp+visudo-cf snippet-staging diagnostic — recovery copy lives in BUSJHL/BUSJHM now, per docket Boundary), three tinder constants (BUJP_sudoers_filename, BUJP_sudoers_mode, BUJP_remote_cache_dir), and seven kindle constants (ZBUJP_SUDO_PROBE_PREFIX, ZBUJP_ADMIN_GROUP_STDOUT/STDERR, ZBUJP_SUDOERS_SNIPPET, ZBUJP_SCP_STDERR, ZBUJP_VISUDO_STDOUT/STDERR). bujp_preflight now two-part: workload shell-environment reachability per letter (zbujp_probe_ssh_connect retained — not host-posture) and invigilate-{platform} dispatch keyed off BURN_PLATFORM; the prior per-letter case block dissolved entirely since the b-letter branch's probes migrated into invigilate and the c|w branch was already empty. Letter-validation preserved by zbujp_probe_ssh_connect's own inner case statement. Caparison-side tightening: caparison's post-completion invigilate check now also asserts sudo/admin-group, so caparison reports complete only when operator-managed precondition posture is wired — appropriate since caparison depends transitively on sudo for sleep-target masking etc.; flagged to operator at mount-time, no pushback. Mac admin-group fact uses dseditgroup stdout-redirected to ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT (membership state lands on stdout per dseditgroup convention). bash -n on touched .sh files; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh 5 fixtures / 28 cases; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 4 fixtures / 98 cases — handbook-render fixture exercises BUS0 includes that pull in BUSJI{L,M} structurally, confirming AsciiDoc-level conformance of the new steps under the locked busc_*/bush_* vocabulary. Remote-node runtime verification of new fact-checks (asserting against a correctly-configured Linux/macOS BURN target) unverified — out of fast-suite tier, deferred to field exercise per ₢A-ABD/ABB analogue.                
57572260  ₢A-ABB    Implement bujb_caparison_{macos,linux} per locked BUSJC{M,L} specs as admin host-posture ceremonies under pre-trusted key auth. macOS: admin SSH probe + sudo -n systemsetup -setremotelogin on + sudo -n pmset -a sleep 0 displaysleep 0 hibernatemode 0 + sudo -n launchctl enable system/com.tailscale.tailscaled + sudo -n launchctl kickstart -k system/com.tailscale.tailscaled + post-completion bujb_invigilate_macos. Linux: admin SSH probe + sudo -n systemctl enable --now sshd + sudo -n systemctl mask sleep.target suspend.target hibernate.target hybrid-sleep.target + sudo -n systemctl enable --now tailscaled + post-completion bujb_invigilate_linux. Each step is one zbujb_admin_exec_native call (WSG SH-10 single-statement); per-step stdout/stderr captured in new ZBUJB_CAPARISON_{STDOUT,STDERR} kindle constants (last sub-step's evidence preserved at die time, matching invigilate's shared-pair pattern). New helper zbujb_caparison_op_die emits uniform fact-shaped diagnostics pointing at operator handbook BUSJH{M,L} scope (caparison is the implementation seat, so failures point outward to operator-managed precondition: ssh-copy-id, sudo NOPASSWD, Tailscale install + first-run auth). BURN_PLATFORM check inlined per function (bubep_mac / bubep_linux) rather than extracting a zbujb_caparison_assert_platform sibling — three-line near-duplicate of zbujb_invigilate_assert_platform did not earn its own helper. CLI wrappers bujb_caparison_{macos,linux}_command in bujb_cli.sh follow the bujb_garrison_bash shape (sentinel + buc_doc_brief + folio guard + resolve_investiture + dispatch). Two zipper enrollments BUWZ_JP_CAPARISON_{MAC,LIN} channel param1 placed adjacent to fenestrate. Two new tabtargets tt/buw-jpC{M,L}.Caparison{Macos,Linux}.sh use the standard launcher line. Mac launchctl approach decision: split into enable + kickstart -k (two calls) rather than launchctl load -w because the latter is non-zero on already-loaded services on modern macOS, breaking idempotency; both enable and kickstart -k are idempotent. If a Mac without Tailscale's launchd plist hits this, kickstart fails with clear 'service not found' pointing operator to BUSJHM (install + first-run auth). Done-when 'run idempotently end-to-end against a Mac and a Linux node' unverified — no live BURN target exercised this run, deferred to field exercise per the analogous deferral in the ABD wrap. 88c32650  ₢A-ABD    Implement bujb_invigilate_{windows,macos,linux} per locked BUSJI{W,M,L} specs — read-only host-posture verification with fact-named diagnostics pointing to operator handbook vs caparison per spec. Each verb opens an admin SSH session, then asserts platform-specific facts: Windows checks two registry values (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion=0 under PasswordLess\Device; PlatformAoAcOverride=0 under Control\Power), Tailscale service presence + StartType=Automatic, powercfg /a reporting neither Standby nor Hibernate available, and WSL distribution BUJB_wsl_distribution registered via wsl --list --quiet. macOS checks systemsetup -getremotelogin On, pmset sleep/displaysleep/hibernatemode all 0 (awk-parsed), launchctl tailscaled label presence + live PID. Linux checks systemctl is-enabled/is-active for sshd and tailscaled (with unit-not-found discrimination routing to handbook vs caparison scope), plus sleep/suspend/hibernate/hybrid-sleep targets all masked. Rewire bujp_preflight into three-part shape: shell-environment reachability per letter (zbujp_probe_ssh_connect retained, not host-posture) + invigilate-{platform} dispatch keyed off BURN_PLATFORM + workload-precondition probes per letter (sudo NOPASSWD + admin-group for letter=b on linux/mac). WSL distribution probe migrated from bujp_preflight to invigilate-windows (subsumed by BUSJIW step 7); zbujp_probe_wsl_distribution function + ZBUJP_WSL_PROBE_STDOUT/STDERR kindle constants deleted. Sudo NOPASSWD + admin-group probes remain inline in bujp as privilege-grant category — locked BUSJI specs do not list them as host-posture facts; follow-on pace slated to extend BUSJI{L,M} and migrate these (operator + spec authority decision discussed at mount-time, Path A chosen over spec-edit-during-implementation Path B). New helpers in bujb_jurisdiction: zbujb_invigilate_assert_platform (BURN_PLATFORM gate), zbujb_invigilate_fact_die (uniform fact:expected:observed:remediation diagnostic shape matching BUSJI axhoc_completion contract); two kindle constants ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT/STDERR (last-fact evidence preserved at die time, matching spec's first-mismatch semantics). Three CLI wrappers (bujb_invigilate_{windows,macos,linux}_command) in bujb_cli.sh + three zipper enrollments (BUWZ_JP_INVIGILATE_{WIN,MAC,LIN}, channel param1, mod bujb_cli.sh) in buwz_zipper.sh + three tabtarget launchers buw-jpI{W,M,L}.Invigilate{Windows,Macos,Linux}.sh — per docket Goal. Mount-time review surfaced that paddock+spec both list only two invigilate invocations (caparison post-completion, garrison precondition) and the standalone operator invocation is not documented as a need; tabtargets/wrappers/enrollments may be dead weight pending operator cleanup decision. Gates green: bash -n on Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh + bujb_cli.sh + bujp_preflight.sh + buwz_zipper.sh + the 3 new tabtarget files; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh (5 fixtures / 28 cases); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh (4 fixtures / 98 cases: 47 enrollment-validation + 27 regime-validation + 9 regime-smoke + 15 handbook-render — the last exercises BUS0 includes that pull in BUSJI{W,M,L} structurally).

40effad0  ₢A-ABE    Close the operator-precondition spec gap surfaced during ₢A-ABD by extending BUSJI{L,M} with handbook-scope precondition facts and migrating the corresponding probes from bujp_preflight into the invigilate verbs. BUSJIL gains two steps after tailscaled-active: sudo NOPASSWD available (`sudo -n true` exit 0) and sudo scope covers garrison commands (`sudo -ln userdel` exit 0), both fatal pointing to BUSJHL scope. BUSJIM gains three steps after tailscaled PID live: admin-group membership (`dseditgroup -o checkmember -m <user> admin`), sudo NOPASSWD available, and sudo scope (`sudo -ln sysadminctl`), all fatal pointing to BUSJHM scope. All new steps follow the existing busc_call/busc_store/busc_require/busc_fatal shape — no new operation kinds introduced. Scope-probe exemplars match garrison-bash's actual sudo callsites in bujb_jurisdiction.sh (userdel for linux, sysadminctl for mac), so the scope assertion is a real future-failure prediction rather than a synthetic check. bujb_invigilate_macos and bujb_invigilate_linux extended with matching zbujb_invigilate_fact_die-shaped fact blocks reusing the function-local z_exit and the ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_{STDOUT,STDERR} kindle constants; pointer strings include BUSJHL/BUSJHM callouts so the diagnostic lands the operator at the recovery copy. bujp_preflight.sh dropped from 309 to ~125 lines: deleted zbujp_probe_sudo, zbujp_probe_admin_group_mac, zbujp_diag_sudo_missing (the elaborate scp+visudo-cf snippet-staging diagnostic — recovery copy lives in BUSJHL/BUSJHM now, per docket Boundary), three tinder constants (BUJP_sudoers_filename, BUJP_sudoers_mode, BUJP_remote_cache_dir), and seven kindle constants (ZBUJP_SUDO_PROBE_PREFIX, ZBUJP_ADMIN_GROUP_STDOUT/STDERR, ZBUJP_SUDOERS_SNIPPET, ZBUJP_SCP_STDERR, ZBUJP_VISUDO_STDOUT/STDERR). bujp_preflight now two-part: workload shell-environment reachability per letter (zbujp_probe_ssh_connect retained — not host-posture) and invigilate-{platform} dispatch keyed off BURN_PLATFORM; the prior per-letter case block dissolved entirely since the b-letter branch's probes migrated into invigilate and the c|w branch was already empty. Letter-validation preserved by zbujp_probe_ssh_connect's own inner case statement. Caparison-side tightening: caparison's post-completion invigilate check now also asserts sudo/admin-group, so caparison reports complete only when operator-managed precondition posture is wired — appropriate since caparison depends transitively on sudo for sleep-target masking etc.; flagged to operator at mount-time, no pushback. 57572260  ₢A-ABB    Implement bujb_caparison_{macos,linux} per locked BUSJC{M,L} specs as admin host-posture ceremonies under pre-trusted key auth. macOS: admin SSH probe + sudo -n systemsetup -setremotelogin on + sudo -n pmset -a sleep 0 displaysleep 0 hibernatemode 0 + sudo -n launchctl enable system/com.tailscale.tailscaled + sudo -n launchctl kickstart -k system/com.tailscale.tailscaled + post-completion bujb_invigilate_macos. Linux: admin SSH probe + sudo -n systemctl enable --now sshd + sudo -n systemctl mask sleep.target suspend.target hibernate.target hybrid-sleep.target + sudo -n systemctl enable --now tailscaled + post-completion bujb_invigilate_linux. Each step is one zbujb_admin_exec_native call (WSG SH-10 single-statement); per-step stdout/stderr captured in new ZBUJB_CAPARISON_{STDOUT,STDERR} kindle constants (last sub-step's evidence preserved at die time, matching invigilate's shared-pair pattern). New helper zbujb_caparison_op_die emits uniform fact-shaped diagnostics pointing at operator handbook BUSJH{M,L} scope (caparison is the implementation seat, so failures point outward to operator-managed precondition: ssh-copy-id, sudo NOPASSWD, Tailscale install + first-run auth). BURN_PLATFORM check inlined per function (bubep_mac / bubep_linux) rather than extracting a zbujb_caparison_assert_platform sibling — three-line near-duplicate of zbujb_invigilate_assert_platform did not earn its own helper. CLI wrappers bujb_caparison_{macos,linux}_command in bujb_cli.sh follow the bujb_garrison_bash shape (sentinel + buc_doc_brief + folio guard + resolve_investiture + dispatch). Mac launchctl approach decision: split into enable + kickstart -k (two calls) rather than launchctl load -w because the latter is non-zero on already-loaded services on modern macOS, breaking idempotency; both enable and kickstart -k are idempotent. If a Mac without Tailscale's launchd plist hits this, kickstart fails with clear 'service not found' pointing operator to BUSJHM (install + first-run auth). content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
7	Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
8	WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
9	integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
10	WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.
11	
12	## Current Concept
13	
14	**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
15	deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
16	its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
17	Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
18	in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.
19	
20	**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**
21	
22	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
23	  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
24	- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
25	  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
26	  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
27	  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
28	  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
29	  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
30	  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.
31	
32	**BUJB constant:**
33	
34	- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
35	  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
36	  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.
37	
38	**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**
39	
40	- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
41	  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
42	  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
43	  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
44	  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
45	  admin-shell operations.
46	- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
47	  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
48	  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
49	  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
50	  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
51	  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
52	  precondition check.
53	- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
54	  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
55	  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
56	  authorized_keys.
57	
58	**Garrison-destructive model:**
59	
60	- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
61	  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
62	- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
63	  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
64	- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
65	  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.
66	
67	**Cross-cutting premises:**
68	
69	- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
70	  it by reference, never duplicates.
71	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
72	  material; operator owns all key administration.
73	
74	**Tabtarget colophons (20 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**
75	
76	Invigilate has no tabtarget — it is invoked only internally (caparison
77	post-completion, garrison precondition via bujp_preflight). The
78	`bujb_invigilate_{windows,macos,linux}` functions exist; no standalone
79	operator invocation.
80	
81	- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
82	- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
83	- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
84	  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
85	- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
86	- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
87	- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
88	- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
89	- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
90	- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3
91	
92	**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**
93	
94	BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
95	implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
96	mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
97	privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
98	distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
99	hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
100	live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.
101	
102	**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**
103	
104	Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
105	admin posture, then post-completion verification.
106	
107	Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):
108	
109	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
110	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
111	   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
112	   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
113	   process never sees the password.
114	2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
115	   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
116	   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
117	   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
118	3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
119	4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
120	   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
121	5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
122	   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
123	   values.
124	6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
125	   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
126	7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
127	   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
128	   expected phase boundary).
129	
130	Phase 2 (key auth only):
131	
132	8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
133	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
134	   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
135	   a session.
136	9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
137	
138	Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):
139	
140	10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
141	    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
142	11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
143	12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
144	    (idempotent).
145	13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
146	    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
147	    wrong.
148	
149	**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**
150	
151	Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
152	ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
153	auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.
154	
155	**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**
156	
157	Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
158	systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
159	invigilate-linux post-check.
160	
161	**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**
162	
163	Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
164	fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
165	and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
166	posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
167	remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
168	garrison's precondition check.
169	
170	**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**
171	
172	1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
173	   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
174	2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
175	   only (no password fallback).
176	3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
177	   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
178	   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
179	4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
180	   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
181	5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
182	   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
183	6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
184	   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
185	   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
186	7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.
187	
188	## Heat Sequence
189	
190	**Spec foundation:**
191	- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
192	- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
193	  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
194	  caparison)
195	
196	**Implementation:**
197	- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
198	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
199	  superseded colophons + handbook entries
200	
201	**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
202	- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
203	  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
204	- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
205	  then macOS/Linux)
206	- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
207	  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)
208	
209	**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
210	- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis
211	
212	**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
213	- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
214	- Garrison-WSL exercise
215	- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
216	- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)
217	
218	## Deferred (named for future return)
219	
220	- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
221	  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
222	  only.
223	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
224	  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
225	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
226	- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
227	  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
228	- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
229	  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
230	- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
231	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
232	- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
233	  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
234	  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
235	  caparison phase or sibling verb.
236	- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
237	  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
238	  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
239	  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
240	  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
241	  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
242	  a time, never both running concurrently).
243	- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
244	  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.
245	
246	## Standing Notes
247	
248	- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
249	  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
250	  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
251	  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
252	  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
253	  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
254	  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
255	  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
256	  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
257	  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
258	  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
259	- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
260	  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
261	  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
262	  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
263	  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
264	- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
265	  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
266	  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
267	  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
268	  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
269	  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
270	- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
271	  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
272	  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
273	  privkey-at-rest protection.
274	- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
275	  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
276	  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
277	  precondition.
278	- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
279	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
280	  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
281	  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
282	- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
283	  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
284	  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
285	  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
286	  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
287	  daemon ever runs concurrently.
288	- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
289	  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
290	  and path construction.
291	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
292	  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
293	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
294	  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
295	- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
296	  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
297	- **Mac Remote Login enabled is an operator prerequisite for any Mac BURN
298	  target** (including the operator's own machine). The first admin SSH
299	  must reach the host before caparison can do anything; only the operator
300	  can flip Remote Login on a fresh machine.
301	- **Linux nodes require `openssh-server` installed and `sshd` enabled** —
302	  operator-managed, not in caparison's scope.
303	- **Sudo NOPASSWD for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is an operator-managed
304	  precondition** wherever garrison needs sudo elevation. On macOS,
305	  admin-group membership for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is also operator-
306	  managed (equivalent in posture to the ssh-copy-id prerequisite).
307	  Blanket `NOPASSWD: ALL` acceptable on personal workstations; scoped
308	  is preferred for shared hosts.
309	- **Workload shell is `/bin/bash` on every platform** regardless of
310	  platform default. macOS ships /bin/bash 3.2 at the canonical path —
311	  bash is the BUK execution substrate everywhere.
312	
313	# jjezs_pace ₢A-ABC
314	
315	## Character
316	Largest implementation pace — fenestrate transition plus new phase 3. Handbook recast moved to ₢A-ABF (parallelizable).
317	
318	## Goal
319	Build caparison-windows verb per BUSJCW: phases 1+2 (SSH-trust hardening, currently inhabited by fenestrate) and phase 3 (WSL stage, powercfg sleep disable, Tailscale auto-start). Delete fenestrate bash implementation.
320	
321	## Boundary
322	- caparison-windows is the new entry; fenestrate disappears from bash: function family, dispatcher, `buw-jpF` tabtarget, zipper enrollment.
323	- Phase 3 ops are new bash; phases 1+2 carry forward existing logic re-homed under the new verb.
324	- Closes with invigilate-windows post-completion check (depends on ₢A-ABD, already landed).
325	- Handbook rewrite is out of scope — owned by ₢A-ABF.
326	
327	## Done when
328	- `buw-jpCW` exists; runs phases 1-3 idempotently end-to-end.
329	- `grep -ri fenestrate` over `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `bujb_cli.sh`, `buwz_zipper.sh`, `bujp_preflight.sh`, and `tt/` returns zero.
330	- bash -n; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh.

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.

## Current Concept

**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.

**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.

**BUJB constant:**

- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.

**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**

- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
  admin-shell operations.
- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
  precondition check.
- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
  authorized_keys.

**Garrison-destructive model:**

- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
  it by reference, never duplicates.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.

**Tabtarget colophons (20 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**

Invigilate has no tabtarget — it is invoked only internally (caparison
post-completion, garrison precondition via bujp_preflight). The
`bujb_invigilate_{windows,macos,linux}` functions exist; no standalone
operator invocation.

- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3

**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**

BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.

**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**

Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
admin posture, then post-completion verification.

Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
   process never sees the password.
2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
   values.
6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
   expected phase boundary).

Phase 2 (key auth only):

8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
   a session.
9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.

Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):

10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
    (idempotent).
13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
    wrong.

**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**

Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.

**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**

Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
invigilate-linux post-check.

**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**

Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
garrison's precondition check.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**

1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
   only (no password fallback).
3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
  caparison)

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
  superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
  then macOS/Linux)
- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
- Garrison-WSL exercise
- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
  caparison phase or sibling verb.
- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
  a time, never both running concurrently).
- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.

## Standing Notes

- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
  privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
  precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
  daemon ever runs concurrently.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
  and path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
- **Mac Remote Login enabled is an operator prerequisite for any Mac BURN
  target** (including the operator's own machine). The first admin SSH
  must reach the host before caparison can do anything; only the operator
  can flip Remote Login on a fresh machine.
- **Linux nodes require `openssh-server` installed and `sshd` enabled** —
  operator-managed, not in caparison's scope.
- **Sudo NOPASSWD for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is an operator-managed
  precondition** wherever garrison needs sudo elevation. On macOS,
  admin-group membership for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is also operator-
  managed (equivalent in posture to the ssh-copy-id prerequisite).
  Blanket `NOPASSWD: ALL` acceptable on personal workstations; scoped
  is preferred for shared hosts.
- **Workload shell is `/bin/bash` on every platform** regardless of
  platform default. macOS ships /bin/bash 3.2 at the canonical path —
  bash is the BUK execution substrate everywhere.

# jjezs_pace ₢A-ABC

## Character
Largest implementation pace — fenestrate transition plus new phase 3. Handbook recast moved to ₢A-ABF (parallelizable).

## Goal
Build caparison-windows verb per BUSJCW: phases 1+2 (SSH-trust hardening, currently inhabited by fenestrate) and phase 3 (WSL stage, powercfg sleep disable, Tailscale auto-start). Delete fenestrate bash implementation.

## Boundary
- caparison-windows is the new entry; fenestrate disappears from bash: function family, dispatcher, `buw-jpF` tabtarget, zipper enrollment.
- Phase 3 ops are new bash; phases 1+2 carry forward existing logic re-homed under the new verb.
- Closes with invigilate-windows post-completion check (depends on ₢A-ABD, already landed).
- Handbook rewrite is out of scope — owned by ₢A-ABF.

## Done when
- `buw-jpCW` exists; runs phases 1-3 idempotently end-to-end.
- `grep -ri fenestrate` over `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `bujb_cli.sh`, `buwz_zipper.sh`, `bujp_preflight.sh`, and `tt/` returns zero.
- bash -n; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh.

content.text:
**Pace goal:** Build `bujb_caparison_windows` per BUSJCW (three phases: SSH-trust hardening + post-trust reconnect + post-trust admin posture), wire `buw-jpCW`, and excise every `fenestrate` reference from bash/dispatcher/zipper/preflight/tt.

**Heat context:** ₣A- (rbk-mvp-3-draft-windows-procedures) — racing, 36/41 done. Paddock framing: jurisdiction → three verbs (caparison / invigilate / garrison); caparison is the admin-posture establishment verb. Recent landings closed the Mac/Linux side: ₢A-ABD landed invigilate-{W,M,L}; ₢A-ABB landed caparison-{mac,linux}; ₢A-ABE extended BUSJI{L,M} with the operator-precondition facts and migrated probes out of `bujp_preflight` into invigilate. This pace is the largest implementation pace in the heat — caparison-windows includes the fenestrate transition (phases 1+2 are existing fenestrate logic re-homed) plus new phase 3.

**Docket:**
- **Character** — Largest implementation pace — fenestrate transition plus new phase 3. Handbook recast moved to ₢A-ABF (parallelizable).
- **Goal** — Build caparison-windows verb per BUSJCW: phases 1+2 (SSH-trust hardening, currently inhabited by fenestrate) and phase 3 (WSL stage, powercfg sleep disable, Tailscale auto-start). Delete fenestrate bash implementation.
- **Boundary** —
  - caparison-windows is the new entry; fenestrate disappears from bash: function family, dispatcher, `buw-jpF` tabtarget, zipper enrollment.
  - Phase 3 ops are new bash; phases 1+2 carry forward existing logic re-homed under the new verb.
  - Closes with invigilate-windows post-completion check (depends on ₢A-ABD, already landed).
  - Handbook rewrite is out of scope — owned by ₢A-ABF.
- **Done when** —
  - `buw-jpCW` exists; runs phases 1-3 idempotently end-to-end.
  - `grep -ri fenestrate` over `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `bujb_cli.sh`, `buwz_zipper.sh`, `bujp_preflight.sh`, and `tt/` returns zero.
  - bash -n; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh.

**Name assessment:** `implement-caparison-windows` fits the docket cleanly — no rename.

Before proposing approach, I need to read the locked BUSJCW spec, the existing fenestrate implementation, and the just-landed caparison-mac/linux shape (for parallel structure). Let me load those.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_caparison_windows}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_caparison} on Windows -- three phases. Phase 1: admin SSH
5	key trust + `sshd_config` hardening. Phase 2: key-only reconnect.
6	Phase 3: post-trust admin posture (WSL distribution, powercfg,
7	Tailscale service). Closes with {bust_invigilate_windows}.
8	
9	NOTE: Nuclear -- every invocation purges and re-establishes the
10	caparison-managed posture; no skip-if-present logic.
11	{bust_handbook_windows} owns operator-manual posture; caparison owns
12	the idempotent admin-shell remainder.
13	
14	NOTE: Phase 1's `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session
15	-- the post-restart disconnect is the expected phase boundary, not a
16	failure. PowerShell-side operations use `Get-Content`, `Set-Service`,
17	`Start-Service`, `Restart-Service`, `wsl.exe --import`; bash-side
18	BCG-compliant matching extracts effective `sshd_config` directives
19	from the raw bytes.
20	
21	The {busn_caparison} operation on Windows:
22	
23	//axhopt_typed_parameter
24	{burp_investiture}
25	
26	//axhoq_precondition
27	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
28	{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node.
29	
30	//axhoq_precondition
31	{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete -- OpenSSH
32	Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
33	applied (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`, `PlatformAoAcOverride`),
34	Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
35	auto-login, and {burp_privileged_user} has a known password.
36	Caparison presupposes this scope.
37	
38	//axhoq_precondition
39	{burp_privileged_user} is in the Administrators group;
40	{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted.
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH session*:
44	.. {busc_call}
45	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
46	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (steady-state: key via
47	{burp_privileged_key_file}; first run: TTY password)
48	.. {busc_store}
49	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` for phase 1 operations
50	.. {busc_fatal}
51	on connection failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Phase 1 — derive admin pubkey*:
55	.. {busc_call}
56	`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_privileged_key_file}
57	.. {busc_store}
58	`«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` from stdout
59	.. {busc_fatal}
60	on derivation failure -- privkey encrypted (unsupported) or unreadable
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Phase 1 — install admin pubkey*:
64	.. write `«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` to `administrators_authorized_keys` over
65	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` as a bare key entry (no `command=`)
66	.. nuclear -- file replaced, not merged
67	.. {busc_fatal}
68	on write failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys ACL*:
72	.. {busc_call}
73	`icacls` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- ACL restricted to SYSTEM and
74	Administrators (Windows OpenSSH refuses the file otherwise);
75	idempotent
76	.. {busc_fatal}
77	on `icacls` failure -- phase 2 reconnect would fail
78	
79	//axhos_step
80	. *Phase 1 — write hardened sshd_config directives*:
81	.. write `PubkeyAuthentication yes`, `PasswordAuthentication no`,
82	`PermitEmptyPasswords no` to `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`;
83	idempotent
84	.. {busc_fatal}
85	on write failure -- restart would still serve password auth
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Phase 1 — verify hardened directives took*:
89	.. {busc_call}
90	PowerShell `Get-Content` on `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`
91	.. {busc_store}
92	`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»`
93	.. {busc_require}
94	`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»` parses to `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
95	`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` as effective
96	directive values (BCG-compliant matching)
97	.. {busc_fatal}
98	on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows
99	
100	//axhos_step
101	. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*:
102	.. {busc_call}
103	`sshd -t` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- penultimate atomic op of phase 1
104	.. {busc_store}
105	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
106	.. {busc_require}
107	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
108	.. {busc_fatal}
109	on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd
110	
111	//axhos_step
112	. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*:
113	.. {busc_call}
114	`Restart-Service sshd` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- final atomic op
115	of phase 1
116	.. {busc_fatal}
117	on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
118	expected phase boundary)
119	
120	//axhos_step
121	. *Phase 2 — reconnect under key-only auth*:
122	.. {busc_call}
123	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
124	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using {burp_privileged_key_file}
125	.. {busc_store}
126	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` for phase 3 operations
127	.. {busc_fatal}
128	on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
129	session); recover via operator's local console
130	
131	//axhos_step
132	. *Phase 3 — stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
133	.. {busc_call}
134	`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over
135	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` to register `rbtww-main` under the admin user
136	(canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction})
137	.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
138	.. {busc_fatal}
139	on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
140	admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted
141	
142	//axhos_step
143	. *Phase 3 — disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
144	.. {busc_call}
145	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
146	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
147	`powercfg /hibernate off` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
148	.. {busc_fatal}
149	on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
150	Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)
151	
152	//axhos_step
153	. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
154	.. {busc_call}
155	`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
156	`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
157	.. {busc_fatal}
158	on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
159	{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)
160	
161	//axhos_step
162	. *Post-completion check — run invigilate-windows*:
163	.. {busc_call}
164	{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}
165	.. {busc_store}
166	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
167	.. {busc_require}
168	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
169	.. {busc_fatal}
170	on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim
171	
172	//axhog_guarantee
173	On success:
174	
175	* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
176	  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
177	* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
178	  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`.
179	* Post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
180	* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user.
181	* `powercfg` sleep and hibernate are disabled.
182	* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
183	* {bust_invigilate_windows} confirms every posture fact end-to-end.
184	
185	//axhoc_completion
186	Exit 0 when the post-completion {bust_invigilate_windows} passes.
187	Non-zero on any phase-1 op failure (admin SSH, pubkey, ACL,
188	`sshd_config`, `sshd -t`), phase-2 reconnect failure, any phase-3 op
189	failure (`wsl --import`, `powercfg`, Tailscale), or post-completion
190	invigilate failure.
191

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
20	#
21	# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
22	# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
23	# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
24	# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
25	# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
26	# hardening directive set.
27	#
28	# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation.
29	
30	set -euo pipefail
31	
32	# Multiple inclusion detection
33	test -z "${ZBUJB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
34	ZBUJB_SOURCED=1
35	
36	# Tinder constants (pure string literals or tinder-on-tinder composition —
37	# available at source time)
38	
39	# Canonical workload OS user name provisioned on every node by garrison.
40	# Project-wide convention; consumed by every account-relative path tinder
41	# below, substituted into BUJB_command_w via bujb_command_for_capture,
42	# and consumed by garrison/cli display strings.
43	BUJB_workload_user='bujuw_user'
44	
45	# Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
46	BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'
47	
48	# wsl.exe invocation prefix for running a bash body as root inside the
49	# canonical workload distribution from any non-bash transport (PS heredoc,
50	# cmd.exe argv). Tinder-on-tinder so a future BUJB_wsl_distribution rename
51	# is a one-line edit. Bare wsl.exe relies on Windows PATH resolution from
52	# system32; the absolute-path form ${BUJB_path_wsl_exe} applies only in
53	# the BUJB_command_w directive where SSH-auth-time resolution requires a
54	# fully qualified path.
55	BUJB_wsl_root_bash_c="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -c"
56	
57	# Path segment shared by every account-relative SSH state directory.
58	BUJB_path_dotssh='.ssh'
59	
60	# authorized_keys basename — OpenSSH-defined filename. Used tinder-on-tinder
61	# in the per-coordinate-system authkeys paths and inline in garrison step4's
62	# authkeys writes.
63	BUJB_authkeys_basename='authorized_keys'
64	
65	# Absolute path to bash on Linux/Mac/WSL — pinned to /bin/bash rather than
66	# the PATH-resolved bare 'bash' for command= directives and user-shell
67	# declarations where the absolute form is required.
68	BUJB_shell_bash='/bin/bash'
69	
70	# POSIX stdin pseudo-device path — used by the install -D pattern in
71	# garrison step5 to plant the workload privkey atomically without any
72	# plaintext temp file on either side's disk.
73	BUJB_path_devstdin='/dev/stdin'
74	
75	# WSL artifact basenames placed under the workload Windows profile by the
76	# garrison-w seed/import sequence.
77	BUJB_seed_basename='rbtww-seed.tar'
78	BUJB_wsl_root_basename='rbtww-fs'
79	
80	# Cygwin install root in two slash conventions. Forward-slash form
81	# survives `command="..."` directives and cmd.exe / PS argv layers
82	# without escape pain (used in BUJB_command_c and zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin).
83	# Back-slash form is for PowerShell path arguments where Test-Path /
84	# Remove-Item expect native Windows separators (used by the obliterate
85	# Cygwin-home probe/remove). Do not collapse — the divergence is
86	# load-bearing per the layer that consumes each form.
87	BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd='C:/cygwin64'
88	BUJB_path_cygwin_root_bs="C:\\cygwin64"
89	
90	# Windows-OpenSSH path to wsl.exe, embedded in the locked-down command=
91	# directive for shell-letter w. Forward-slash form per the same argv-layer
92	# rule as BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd.
93	BUJB_path_wsl_exe='C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe'
94	
95	# Workload home directories per coordinate system. Tinder-on-tinder so a
96	# future BUJB_workload_user rename is a one-line edit.
97	BUJB_path_win_user_home="C:\\Users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
98	BUJB_path_winenv_user_home="%SystemDrive%\\Users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
99	BUJB_path_wsl_user_home="/mnt/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
100	BUJB_path_cyg_user_home="/cygdrive/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
101	BUJB_path_mac_user_home="/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
102	BUJB_path_posix_user_home="/home/${BUJB_workload_user}"
103	BUJB_path_cygwin_user_home="${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_bs}\\home\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
104	
105	# .ssh directories under each home.
106	BUJB_path_win_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
107	BUJB_path_wsl_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_wsl_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
108	BUJB_path_cyg_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_cyg_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
109	BUJB_path_posix_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
110	
111	# authorized_keys file under each .ssh.
112	BUJB_path_win_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_win_user_ssh}\\${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
113	BUJB_path_wsl_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_wsl_user_ssh}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
114	BUJB_path_cyg_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_cyg_user_ssh}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
115	
116	# WSL artifacts under the Windows workload profile.
117	BUJB_path_win_seed_tarball="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_seed_basename}"
118	BUJB_path_win_wsl_root="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_wsl_root_basename}"
119	
120	# WSL-install seed distribution: the Microsoft-published distribution that
121	# bujb_wsl_install fetches via `wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d`, exports
122	# to .tar, then re-imports under BUJB_wsl_distribution.
123	BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution='Ubuntu-24.04'
124	
125	# Windows-side install directory housing both seed and canonical
126	# distribution VHD trees plus the intermediate .tar export.
127	BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root='C:\WSL'
128	
129	# Windows ACL principal names used in icacls /grant arguments. The :F
130	# permission suffix is appended at use sites alongside ${BUJB_workload_user}:F
131	# so all three principals follow the same `<principal>:F` shape.
132	BUJB_acl_principal_system='SYSTEM'
133	BUJB_acl_principal_admins='BUILTIN\Administrators'
134	
135	# Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
136	# Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
137	# behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
138	# requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
139	# Tinder-on-tinder source-time interpolation: cygwin/wsl/distribution/user
140	# resolve at source time so the value is a fully-baked literal by the time
141	# bujb_command_for_capture echoes it. \"...\" pairs are literal escaped
142	# quotes the remote sshd parser expects around the command directive's
143	# argument; \$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND defers expansion to the remote shell.
144	BUJB_command_b="command=\"${BUJB_shell_bash} -lc \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"
145	BUJB_command_c="command=\"${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd}/bin/bash --login -c \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"
146	BUJB_command_w="command=\"${BUJB_path_wsl_exe} --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user ${BUJB_workload_user} --exec ${BUJB_shell_bash} -lc \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"
147	
148	# Workload privkey destination path on the remote (relative to the workload
149	# account home directory). Uniform across shell-letters under the current
150	# design — if a future shell needs a divergent keypath, fan out per-letter.
151	BUJB_workload_keypath='.ssh/id_ed25519'
152	
153	# Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
154	# fenestrate phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
155	# bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.
156	BUJB_sshd_hardening='PubkeyAuthentication yes
157	PasswordAuthentication no
158	PermitEmptyPasswords no'
159	
160	# User-provisioning argument sets per platform tooling.
161	#
162	# BUJB_useradd_workload_args — Linux/WSL useradd flags declaring the
163	# workload account policy (home-directory creation + absolute shell).
164	# Used in step3 b (linux), step3 b (mac via dscl UserShell), and
165	# w_init wsl-useradd.
166	# BUJB_netuser_add_args — Windows net.exe user /add flags declaring the
167	# password-not-required + active workload posture (used in step3 c and
168	# step3 w).
169	BUJB_useradd_workload_args="--create-home --shell ${BUJB_shell_bash}"
170	BUJB_netuser_add_args='/add /passwordreq:no /active:yes'
171	
172	# ssh-keygen invocation prefix for emitting the public key derived from a
173	# private key under empty-passphrase dry-load. Idiom used for admin-key
174	# resolve-investiture validation and for workload/admin pubkey emission in
175	# garrison step4, w-lockdown, and fenestrate phase 1.
176	BUJB_sshkeygen_emit_pubkey="ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f"
177	
178	# SSH option values used inline at every ssh call alongside
179	# ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS. BatchMode=yes / ConnectTimeout=15 is the dominant
180	# pair; fenestrate's password-fallback case uses BatchMode=no inline and
181	# step6_validate's knock uses ConnectTimeout=10 inline.
182	BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes='BatchMode=yes'
183	BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15='ConnectTimeout=15'
184	
185	# PowerShell CLI invocation forms. _command for one-shot string invocations
186	# (the dominant case); _file_stdin for streaming a PS script body from
187	# stdin (fenestrate phases).
188	BUJB_ps_invoke_command='powershell -NoProfile -Command'
189	BUJB_ps_invoke_file_stdin='powershell -NoProfile -File -'
190	
191	# PowerShell session discipline prelude shared by admin_powershell and
192	# powershell_capture:
193	#   $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'  — PS errors terminate the session.
194	#   $env:WSL_UTF8 = 1                — wsl.exe emits UTF-8 (not UTF-16).
195	#   $LASTEXITCODE = 0                — initialize so a trailing if-check
196	#     does not trip on the $null default ($null -ne 0 evaluates True in
197	#     PowerShell typed comparison, which would fire `exit $null` and
198	#     discard buffered object-formatter output for cmdlets like
199	#     Get-LocalUser whose tables have not flushed to stdout yet).
200	BUJB_ps_prelude="\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; \$env:WSL_UTF8 = 1; \$LASTEXITCODE = 0;"
201	
202	# PowerShell-form path to the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config. Bash-side
203	# substitution at the use site expands this tinder, baking the literal
204	# $env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config into the constructed PS body.
205	BUJB_ps_sshd_config_path='$env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config'
206	
207	# Windows registry subpath under HKLM for the per-SID ProfileList key
208	# created by step3 w to register the workload user's profile with Windows.
209	# Two consumers prefix it differently: PS-drive form 'HKLM:' for
210	# New-Item / New-ItemProperty, and reg.exe hive form 'HKLM' for the
211	# validate-diag dump.
212	BUJB_winreg_profilelist_subpath='SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileList'
213	
214	######################################################################
215	# Internal Functions (zbujb_*)
216	
217	zbujb_kindle() {
218	  test -z "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb already kindled"
219	
220	  # Fenestrate temp file paths — ssh stdout/stderr captured here so
221	  # callers parse from disk (no `$(ssh ...)` capture).
222	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stdout.txt"
223	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stderr.txt"
224	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stdout.txt"
225	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stderr.txt"
226	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stdout.txt"
227	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stderr.txt"
228	
229	  # stat captures (per-key-slot for parallel forensics on resolve).
230	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_priv_stdout.txt"
231	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_priv_stderr.txt"
232	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_work_stdout.txt"
233	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_work_stderr.txt"
234	
235	  # ssh-keygen dry-load captures (resolve-time, per slot).
236	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_priv_stdout.txt"
237	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_priv_stderr.txt"
238	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_work_stdout.txt"
239	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_work_stderr.txt"
240	
241	  # ssh-keygen pubkey-emit captures (garrison step4 = workload pubkey,
242	  # fenestrate phase1 = admin pubkey).
243	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_work_stdout.txt"
244	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_work_stderr.txt"
245	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_priv_stdout.txt"
246	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_priv_stderr.txt"
247	
248	  # base64 capture (garrison step4 w-branch = authorized_keys line b64-encoded
249	  # for transport to remote).
250	  readonly ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_authkeys_b64_stdout.txt"
251	  readonly ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_authkeys_b64_stderr.txt"
252	
253	  # Obliterate per-call captures (reused across the Windows sub-step
254	  # sequence — last call's content is what's preserved at die time).
255	  readonly ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_obliterate_stdout.txt"
256	  readonly ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_obliterate_stderr.txt"
257	
258	  # Output capture paths for bujb_command_file (workload stdout/stderr/exit).
259	  readonly ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDOUT="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stdout.log"
260	  readonly ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"
